name: Smith Ortiz, P.C.
description_short: Chicago bankruptcy law firm with 25+ years of shared experience handling Chapter 7, Chapter 11, and Chapter 13 cases for individuals and businesses.
description_long:
Smith Ortiz, P.C. is a Chicago-area bankruptcy and debt relief law firm serving the Chicagoland region. The firm was founded by attorneys with more than 25 years of combined hands-on experience in bankruptcy and debt relief law, and one of its founders holds both a law degree and an MBA, giving the practice a business-oriented perspective on financial distress cases. The firm advertises having helped hundreds of clients achieve favorable bankruptcy outcomes and positions itself as a full-service legal resource for consumers and business owners facing financial hardship.

The firm handles the full spectrum of consumer and business bankruptcy chapters, including Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcy, Chapter 13 repayment-plan bankruptcy, and Chapter 11 business reorganization. Beyond bankruptcy filings, Smith Ortiz also provides credit restoration services, debt collection defense, foreclosure defense, assistance with repossession matters, and wage garnishment defense. The firm offers a free initial case evaluation to assess each prospective client's situation before any commitment is made.

What distinguishes Smith Ortiz is its stated emphasis on individual attention — the firm describes conducting a full financial review for every client before recommending a course of action. The combination of legal and MBA credentials among its founders is a notable differentiator for clients with complex business-related debt situations. Client testimonials on the website reflect positive experiences with communication, patience, and thoroughness throughout the bankruptcy process.

Honestly assessed, Smith Ortiz is a legitimate, experienced bankruptcy law firm serving a specific geographic market. However, the website provides limited transparency about individual attorney backgrounds beyond the brief credential mentions, and the credit restoration service is listed without any detail about what it actually entails. One client review references divorce representation, suggesting the firm may handle matters outside pure bankruptcy — worth clarifying during a consultation. Services are geographically limited to the Chicago area.
pros: 25+ years of combined attorney experience specifically in bankruptcy and debt relief
One founder holds both a J.D. and an MBA, offering business-informed legal strategy
Handles all three major bankruptcy chapters: 7, 11, and 13
Free initial case evaluation offered before any commitment
Individual client review process conducted before recommending a bankruptcy strategy
Offers foreclosure defense and wage garnishment defense alongside bankruptcy filings
Multiple client testimonials cite clear step-by-step explanations throughout the process
cons: Services limited to Chicago and Chicagoland — not available to clients outside Illinois
Credit restoration is listed as a service but no details about methodology or scope are provided on the website
Individual attorney bios and bar credentials are not prominently displayed on the website
No transparent fee structure or pricing information published online
At least one client review references divorce representation, suggesting an unclear practice scope that may dilute bankruptcy specialization
best_for: Chicago-area residents with significant unsecured debt seeking a Chapter 7 discharge
Illinois homeowners facing foreclosure who need legal defense alongside bankruptcy consideration
Workers subject to active wage garnishment seeking immediate legal intervention
Small business owners in the Chicagoland area exploring Chapter 11 reorganization
diagnosis: Smith Ortiz, P.C. is best suited for Chicago-area individuals and small business owners facing serious debt crises — particularly those dealing with foreclosure, wage garnishment, or large unsecured debt loads who need a full-service bankruptcy attorney rather than a document preparation service. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Illinois and a lack of published fee information, so prospective clients outside the Chicago metro area or those comparing costs upfront will need to look elsewhere or call directly.
services: Means test analysis for Chapter 7 qualification
